question: What is the volume of the cylindrical section of the PZR region?

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and supports various reactor components, with its materials and fabrication adhering to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), emphasizing its integration with the RPV and its role in system operations.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures to ensure the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel. Key standards cited include the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code and ASME NQA-1 for quality assurance.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how DHRS actuation valves are tested during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the procedures for valve exercising and position verification, and specifies that a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

edges.description: Section A describes the function and operational context of the PZR region within the RCS, while Section B provides the physical configuration and components of the reactor vessel that contains the PZR, connecting them through the shared system (RCS) and the location of the PZR within the RPV.

Section A describes the function and operation of the PZR volume and its degasification line, while Section B details operational steps involving the PZR water level and venting processes, both referencing the PZR region and its management but neither providing the specific volume value requested in the analysis question.

Section A describes the operational role and connections of the PZR region within the RCS, while Section B provides the physical configuration and boundaries of the pressurizer region, which are necessary to determine the volume of its cylindrical section.

Section A describes the overall structure and components of the reactor vessel, which includes the pressurizer (PZR), while Section B provides operational details about the PZR water level and pressure management, connecting the physical component described in A to its functional operation relevant for determining the PZR region's volume.

Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral part of the reactor vessel described in Section A, detailing that it comprises the upper region of the vessel above the circulating coolant and is separated by a baffle plate.
Section B references Section A by discussing inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the Reactor Vessel described in Section A, connecting them through the shared subject of the RPV.

Section B defines the pressurizer region as the upper part of the reactor vessel, which provides the necessary spatial context for identifying the cylindrical section whose volume is being asked about in the analysis question, while Section A describes operational procedures involving the PZR but does not specify its geometry.

Section A and Section B both discuss inspection and testing requirements for reactor vessel and reactor coolant system components, referencing compliance with ASME BPVC codes, thereby connecting through shared regulatory standards for component integrity.
